statement: The system shall provide multiple hardware emergency stops accessible at several positions around the operator workstation (e.g. behind the head, by the feet, by the arms).
The system shall automatically trip to a safe state when the arm exerts excessive force against an obstacle or resistance.
The system shall support pre-programmed arm movements via teach files, allowing the robot to move autonomously to predefined positions on command.
The system shall allow the operator to pause or immediately stop a pre-programmed arm move while it is executing.
The system shall display a ghost preview showing the predicted end position of a commanded movement, and the intermediate waypoints along the planned path, before the move is executed.
When a large pre-programmed move is commanded, the system shall show the full routing path around obstacles, not just the final endpoint, so the operator can verify the trajectory before confirming.
The system shall provide at least two camera views simultaneously: one fixed overhead overview for global spatial reference and at least one adjustable camera for close-up work.
The system shall provide a camera auto-follow mode that pans and tracks the arm's position during operation, configurable per operator preference.
The system shall prioritise frame rate over resolution for camera streams used during active manipulation tasks.
The system shall support configurable no-go zones defined as rectangular regions or spherical exclusion volumes that physically prevent the robot from entering.
The system shall emit an audible alarm when gripper force exceeds a configured threshold, to warn the operator against over-gripping.
The system shall provide adjustable force-ratio (load compensation) settings that reduce the apparent weight of heavy objects being manipulated.
The system shall maintain haptic force feedback sensitivity independent of the force-ratio setting, so operators can handle heavy objects while retaining fine tactile perception.
